skill.name: query-builder
skill.description: Generate, optimize, and explain SQL, NoSQL, and ORM database queries for common application data tasks.
skill.summary: Build readable database queries with performance guidance and safer parameterized patterns.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. AI review found these are false positives caused by Markdown fences and database query examples, with no executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or secret handling found.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fence delimiters around usage, SQL, TypeScript, Python, and JavaScript examples. They are documentation examples, not executable commands or runtime shell invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fenced code blocks in a single documentation file. No shell command execution API or instruction to execute local commands appears in those ranges.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are SQL Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptographic algorithm detections occur inside normal skill metadata and database query examples, such as ORDER BY clauses and SQL functions. No cryptographic implementation, hash choice, key handling, or security-sensitive encryption workflow was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is instructional database content and the cited lines are not cryptographic code. The scanner appears to have matched short substrings inside unrelated prose or SQL syntax.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Findings Are Query Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance detections are SQL, ORM, and NoSQL examples that reference tables, joins, indexes, pagination, and aggregation. No OS discovery, host enumeration, file probing, credential discovery, or environment inspection behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is normal database query documentation. It does not instruct the model to inspect the host system or collect local system information.

low_findings.title: Database Query Generation Has Inherent Data Safety Risk
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE as a domain risk, not malicious behavior. The skill teaches SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, ALTER TABLE, index creation, and trigger patterns. Users should review generated queries before applying them to production data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill clearly includes data-changing and schema-changing database examples. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but mistakes could affect data integrity if copied without review.

content.value_statement: Teams often need correct queries across several database engines and ORMs. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code generate readable queries with safer parameters and performance notes.
actual_capabilities: Generates SQL queries for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, SQLite, and SQL Server.
Creates NoSQL examples for MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Cassandra patterns.
Provides ORM query examples for Prisma, TypeORM, Sequelize, SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, and Mongoose.
Explains joins, aggregations, subqueries, CTEs, pagination, and upsert patterns.
Suggests indexes and query rewrites for common performance issues.
Encourages parameterized queries to reduce SQL injection risk.
limitations: Does not connect to a database or validate queries against a live schema.
Cannot guarantee execution plans without real data volume and indexes.
May need dialect adjustments for vendor-specific SQL behavior.
Generated write queries should be reviewed before production use.

use_cases.title: Create Application Queries
use_cases.description: Generate readable database queries from feature requirements and table relationships.

use_cases.title: Improve Slow Reports
use_cases.description: Rewrite reporting queries with better filters, joins, indexes, and pagination patterns.

use_cases.title: Translate ORM Patterns
use_cases.description: Convert between raw SQL and ORM query builder examples for application code.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Basic Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a simple data retrieval need.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use query-builder to create a query for this request: [describe rows, filters, and sort order]. Use [database type].

prompt_templates.title: Add Joins and Aggregates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use query-builder to join these tables: [tables and relationships]. Return the grouped metrics I need: [metrics].

prompt_templates.title: Optimize an Existing Query
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve performance of a query that is already written.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use query-builder to review this query for performance: [paste query]. Explain indexes, rewrites, and pagination options.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Raw SQL and ORM Versions
prompt_templates.scenario: Create production-ready examples for application code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use query-builder to generate raw SQL and a [Prisma, TypeORM, SQLAlchemy, or Mongoose] version for this requirement: [requirement]. Use parameters.

output_examples.input: Find active users with completed orders from the last 30 days.
output_examples.output: A joined query with user and order fields, date filtering, completed status filtering, sorted results, and recommended indexes.

output_examples.input: Optimize a report that groups revenue by day.
output_examples.output: A grouped reporting query, an explanation of date aggregation, index suggestions, and notes about filtering before grouping.

output_examples.input: Convert a user lookup into an ORM query.
output_examples.output: An ORM query pattern with parameterized filters, relation loading guidance, and a raw SQL comparison.

best_practices: Provide the database engine, table names, relationships, and expected result shape.
Ask for parameterized output when queries include user-supplied values.
Review generated write queries and test them with realistic data before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad queries without explaining filters, limits, or result columns.
Do not run generated schema changes without a migration and rollback plan.
Do not assume index recommendations are correct without checking the real execution plan.

faq.question: Can this skill run queries against my database?
faq.answer: No. It provides query text, explanations, and optimization guidance. It does not connect to a database.

faq.question: Does it support both SQL and NoSQL databases?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers common SQL engines and NoSQL patterns for MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Cassandra.

faq.question: Can it generate ORM code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for Prisma, TypeORM, Sequelize, SQLAlchemy, Django ORM, and Mongoose.

faq.question: Does it help prevent SQL injection?
faq.answer: It encourages parameterized queries and can format examples with placeholders. Users still need application-level validation.

faq.question: Can it optimize slow queries?
faq.answer: It can suggest rewrites, indexes, and pagination patterns. Final tuning requires real execution plans and production-like data.

faq.question: Is it safe for production database changes?
faq.answer: Generated data-changing or schema-changing queries need review, testing, backups, and normal migration controls.
